agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Sign Bylaw update to regulate digital sign brightness
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is considering an update to Burnaby's Sign Bylaw to create new rules for digital advertising signs, following a resident complaint about a bright Pattison display at Gilmore Place facing Dawson Street. The resident reports the sign, activated March 4, 2026, remains visible even with blinds closed and may not be dimming appropriately at night. The bylaw update is expected to address brightness levels and glare standards.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on the Sign Bylaw update — it could affect your existing or planned digital sign.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
1.1
Kristi Kendell - Bright Digital Advertising Sign at Gilmore Place
1.
Kristi Kendell - Bright Digital Advertising Sign at Gilmore Place Facing Dawson St.pdf
Copy: Chief Administrative Officer, General Manager Corporate Services, General Manager Community Safety, General Manager Planning and Development
Note from Acting General Manager Planning and Development:
The Gilmore Place digital sign was installed in accordance with approved signage plans but was initially illuminated at a high level that prompted neighbourhood complaints and public attention. While the brightness has since been reduced, City staff remain in contact with the owner to further address concerns raised.  It is noted that digital sign regulations are currently being reviewed and advanced through a forthcoming Sign Bylaw update.

attachments.title: Kristi Kendell - Bright Digital Advertising Sign at Gilmore Place Facing Dawson

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — April 28, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby police service fees under review after complaint
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is considering a review of police service fees under Bylaw 14485C, Schedule A7 after Lise Magnan and Kirk Daniells were charged $70 each for police record checks to host two Canadian youth for a one-week Experiences Canada exchange. The complainants argue the bylaw was unfairly applied because they receive no financial benefit, unlike a paid homestay. The RCMP detachment will re-examine the fee decision at the supervisor/manager level. No changes have been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed now. Watch for the council decision and any follow-up from the RCMP review.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
1.2
Lise Magnan and Kirk Daniells - Police Service Fees (Bylaw 14485C)
1.
Lise Magnan and Kirk Daniells - City of Burnaby Bylaw 14485C, Schedule A7 Police Service Fees.pdf
Copy: Chief Administrative Officer, General Manager Corporate Services, General Manager Community Safety
Note from General Manager Community Safety:
The letter and attachments from Lisa Magnan and Kirk Daniells have been forwarded to staff at the RCMP detachment to review the circumstances of this decision.  This will be assessed at the supervisor/manager level to ensure the appropriate decision is made and follow up with the writers will be undertaken to ensure proper clarity and understanding of the resulting assessment and decision.

attachments.title: Lise Magnan and Kirk Daniells - City of Burnaby Bylaw 14485C, Schedule A7 Police

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — April 28, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Parking meter rates doubled on 18th Avenue near Edmonds
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposal to double the parking meter rates on 18th Avenue near the Edmonds SkyTrain Station. The proposed bylaw would increase the cost of on-street pay parking in this area to encourage more people to use transit, walking, or cycling instead of driving. The city says the higher rate is meant to balance the needs of local residents and transit users while promoting active transportation.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, parking costs on 18th Avenue near Edmonds SkyTrain will double.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
1.3
Nazreen Jakir - Parking Meter Rates on 18th Avenue by Edmonds Skytrain Doubling
1.
Nazreen Jakir - Parking Meter Rates on 18th Avenue by Edmonds Skytrain Doubling.pdf
Copy
:
Chief Administrative Officer, General Manager Corporate Services, General Manager Engineering
Note from General Manager Engineering
: The City’s goal is to encourage multi-modal transportation around the Edmonds SkyTrain Station and reduce reliance on private cars. It is recognized that permitting long-term public on-street pay parking around Edmonds SkyTrain Station bridges the gap for public transit. With extensive data collection over the years to allow parking for Edmonds residents and SkyTrain users, the updated parking rate reflects a balance between accessibility and community needs while also supporting active transportation as the preferred mode of travel.

attachments.title: Nazreen Jakir - Parking Meter Rates on 18th Avenue by Edmonds Skytrain Doubling.

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — April 28, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to consider non-market housing concentration on Byrnepark Drive
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to consider a report addressing concerns about the concentration of non-market housing on Byrnepark Drive. The report notes that these developments follow long-range planning policies and the Edmonds Community Plan, which included public hearings and community input. The proposed housing supports Burnaby's HOME Strategy and aims to meet the city's identified housing needs. No final decision has been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision and any future public hearing dates — this could affect property development and community planning in the area.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
1.4
Vasu Dadhania - Concerns Regarding Concentration of Non-Market Housing on Byrnepark Drive
1.
Vasu Dadhania - Concerns Regarding Concentration of Non-Market Housing on Byrnepark Drive.pdf
Copy: Chief Administrative Officer, General Manager Corporate Services, General Manager Planning and Development
Note from Acting General Manager Planning and Development
: These developments have been guided by long‑range planning policies and have included opportunities for public input. Consultation was undertaken through public hearings, as well as through the Edmonds Community Plan process, which established the overall land use framework and housing objectives for the area. These developments support the HOME Strategy and are intended to help address Burnaby's identified housing needs, consistent with Council-approved policy direction.

attachments.title: Vasu Dadhania - Concerns Regarding Concentration of Non-Market Housing on Byrnep

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — April 28, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Metro Vancouver 10-Year Housing Plan received by Burnaby Council
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has received Metro Vancouver's proposed 10-Year Housing Plan for information. The plan sets specific targets: 2,000 new or redeveloped homes over 10 years (200/year), minimum 70% two-bedroom or larger units portfolio-wide, minimum 30% rent-geared-to-income or deeper affordability, and 5% accessible units in all new buildings. The portfolio must be 100% carbon neutral by 2050 with a 25% energy reduction through major retrofits. Council will consider the plan at a future meeting before deciding whether to support it.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
3.1.2
Housing 10-Year Plan
1.

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — April 28, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Zoning amendment for 727 North Road, Coquitlam
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider Bylaw No. 5527, 2026 to rezone 727 North Road from RM-2 (three-storey medium density apartment residential) to CD-59 Comprehensive Development Zone. If approved, the amendment would allow a six-storey rental building and a 27-storey residential tower on two lots linked by a shared site covenant. Written comments must be received by noon on April 27, 2026.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Submit written comments to clerks@coquitlam.ca with subject line 'Zoning Amendment - PROJ 23-025' or deliver to Legislative Services Division by noon on April 27, 2026.

key_dates.label: Comment period closes — Coquitlam zoning amendment 727 North Road
Public hearing — Coquitlam zoning amendment 727 North Road

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — April 28, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Permanent daylight-saving time impact on school travel safety
agenda_briefs.summary: Langford City Council passed a resolution on March 30, 2026, urging the Province to reopen the BC Active Transportation Infrastructure Grant Program for safe routes to school projects, establish a program providing students with reflective gear and lights, and expand the Walking School Bus program. This responds to safety risks from permanent daylight-saving time, which will cause later winter sunrises and darker morning commutes for students, especially in Sooke School District 62.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Chemtrade to present operations info to Burnaby Council
agenda_briefs.summary: Chemtrade requests to present information to Burnaby Council about its North Vancouver chlor-alkali facility, which produces 70% of liquid chlorine for Western Canada and 40% for Canada. The facility has operated safely since 1957, employs 118 workers, and has proposed safety upgrades including removal of on-site chlorine storage and construction of an enclosed railcar loading building. The District of North Vancouver council is scheduled to vote on required rezoning bylaw changes next week.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No immediate action needed. Watch for the Burnaby Council meeting date if you want to attend or submit comments.

key_dates.label: District of North Vancouver rezoning bylaw vote

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to consider closure of Michael J. Fox Theatre
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a proposal regarding the closure of the Michael J. Fox Theatre. The Chief Administrative Officer and General Managers of Corporate Services and Parks, Recreation and Culture have submitted correspondence on this matter. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been approved or enacted. Business owners and community members should be aware that the future of this city-owned theatre is up for discussion at an upcoming Council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council meeting date and attend or submit comments to have your say on the proposed theatre closure.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

Closure of Michael J. Fox Theatre
Copy: Chief Administrative Officer, General Manager Corporate Services, General Manager Parks, Recreation and Culture

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby-North Shore Rapid Bus Transit Project correspondence
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider correspondence from Janice Branson regarding the proposed Burnaby-North Shore Rapid Bus Transit Project. This is a proposed rapid bus line that would connect Burnaby to the North Shore. The correspondence is part of the public record and council will review it before making any decisions on the project. No vote has been taken yet — this is an item up for discussion at an upcoming council meeting.

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — April 28, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on Street and Traffic Bylaw amendments
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on proposed amendments to Burnaby's Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961. The changes, put forward by Markus Lau, would update rules for street use and traffic in the city. A bylaw amendment is a formal change to an existing local law. The proposal has not been approved yet — council will consider it at an upcoming meeting. Business owners who use streets for loading, parking, or events should watch for the council decision, as it could affect permits and operations.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and decision — this could affect your street-use permits or traffic-related operations.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Purpose-built rental student housing at Rathburn Drive (Lougheed Town Centre)
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed rezoning (Rez #25-19) for a purpose-built multiple-family rental development at Rathburn Drive in the Lougheed Town Centre area. The development is intended specifically for student accommodation. If approved, this would create new rental housing designed for students. The proposal is part of the Lougheed Town Centre Plan and is currently under consideration — it has not yet been approved or enacted.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit written comments to Burnaby Council to voice your support or concerns before the vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — April 28, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Pedestrian scramble intersection at Metrotown
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposal to build a pedestrian scramble intersection at Metrotown. A pedestrian scramble is a crosswalk that lets people walk in all directions — including diagonally — at the same time, while all vehicle traffic is stopped. If approved, this change would affect traffic flow and pedestrian safety near the busy shopping area. The decision has not been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect traffic and access near your business at Metrotown.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on rezoning at 6535 Kingsway
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will consider first and second reading of Rezoning Bylaw No. 14809 (REZ #25-09) on April 28, 2026 at 5 pm. The proposal rezones 6535 Kingsway from C7 Drive-In Restaurant to CD Comprehensive Development District to permit a 12-storey mixed-use building with 290 residential units, double-height commercial fronting Kingsway, and underground parking. No public hearing will be held; written comments must include the author's legal name and civic address.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Submit written comments referencing REZ #25-09 by 12:00 noon on Tuesday, April 21, 2026 to be included in the Council package.

key_dates.label: Council first and second reading — REZ #25-09 Burnaby
Written comment deadline for Council package — REZ #25-09
